# Contract drift CI

> Theme: [Federation](../README.md)

## Purpose

Every pillar owns its wire contract in `pillars/<id>/src/contract/` and emits three deterministic artifacts from it: an OpenAPI document, a generated TypeScript types module, and a static manifest module. Those artifacts are committed to git so that polyglot consumers, the module registry, and downstream typecheckers can read them without running a build.

The hazard is **drift**: a contract author edits `src/contract/` but forgets to regenerate (or commits a hand-edited artifact), so the committed OpenAPI / types / manifest no longer match the source. A consumer then typechecks green against a stale surface and breaks at runtime.

This PRD is the CI gate that makes that impossible: for every changed unit, CI re-runs the unit's `generate:*` scripts and hard-fails on any diff between the freshly generated output and what the PR committed. The committed artifact is forced to be a pure projection of the source contract, on every PR.

## Contract artifacts (committed to git)

Each pillar that serves a contract carries, under `pillars/<id>/`:

```
pillars/<id>/
├── src/contract/
│   ├── rest.ts                    # the ts-rest + zod contract — canonical source
│   ├── manifest.ts                # hand-authored manifest source
│   ├── manifest.generated.ts      # GENERATED, committed — only where the pillar declares generate:manifest
│   ├── api-types.generated.ts     # GENERATED, committed — TS types from the OpenAPI doc
│   └── index.ts
├── openapi/<id>.openapi.json       # GENERATED, committed — OpenAPI 3.0 projection of rest.ts
└── package.json                    # exports ".", "./manifest", "./api-types", "./openapi"
```

Every TS contract pillar carries the OpenAPI document and `api-types.generated.ts` and declares the `generate:openapi` + `generate:api-types` scripts that re-derive them, so both are universally drift-gated. `manifest.generated.ts` is a projection only for pillars that declare a `generate:manifest` script (`cerebrum`, `food`, `inventory`, `lists`); the others either author the manifest by hand (`ai`) or carry a legacy committed `manifest.generated.ts` that no live generator reproduces (`finance`, `media`, `registry`) — only the artifacts a pillar declares a `generate:*` script for are drift-gated.

Rust pillars (e.g. `contacts`) emit their OpenAPI document from `axum` rather than `ts-rest`; the same committed-artifact + drift-gate rule applies.

### Generation is a pure projection

The generators are deterministic so the regenerate-and-diff check is stable:

- Output keys are recursively sorted, then passed through the repo formatter (`oxfmt`).
- No timestamps, no machine-specific paths, no nondeterministic ordering.
- `generate-openapi.ts` projects `src/contract/rest.ts` → `openapi/<id>.openapi.json` (every TS contract pillar).
- `generate-api-types.ts` feeds that OpenAPI JSON through `openapi-typescript` → `src/contract/api-types.generated.ts` (every TS contract pillar).
- Where a pillar declares `generate:manifest`, manifest generation projects the contract into `manifest.generated.ts` (`cerebrum`, `food`, `inventory`, `lists`).

The `module-registry` lib has its own equivalent: `generated.ts` is regenerated by `mise run registry` (which imports the pillars' built `dist/` manifests) and diff-gated separately.

## CI surface

### `generate:*` drift gate — `unit-quality.yml` (per PR)

`.github/workflows/unit-quality.yml` runs a single disk-discovered matrix over every buildable unit under `pillars/` and `libs/` (maxdepth 1, TS + Rust). For each TS unit it runs the **Codegen drift** step:

1. Enumerate the unit's `package.json` scripts whose name starts with `generate:`.
2. Run each one via `pnpm --filter <pkg> <script>`.
3. `git diff --exit-code <unit-dir>/` — any change fails the job.

Units with no `generate:*` scripts skip the step. A failure prints which artifacts drifted; the fix is to run the unit's `generate:*` scripts locally and commit the result.

### Affected-unit selection — `_discover-units.yml`

`.github/workflows/_discover-units.yml` is a reusable workflow that computes the **changed set** the matrix runs against:

- It scans `pillars/*` + `libs/*` (maxdepth 1) for units carrying a `package.json` (or `Cargo.toml`), keyed by the manifest `name` (NOT the dir basename — selecting on basename silently matches zero packages).
- The changed set is `git diff --name-only <merge-base> HEAD`, where `<merge-base> = git merge-base origin/<base_ref> HEAD`. Anchoring to the PR's merge-base (not `main`'s tip) keeps the diff stable when `main` advances mid-PR.
- A touch to any **shared root** (`pnpm-lock.yaml`, `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, `tsconfig.base.json`, `tsconfig.build.json`, `.oxfmtrc.json`, `.oxlintrc.json`, `mise.toml`, `mise.ci.toml`, `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`) invalidates **all** units.
- `_discover-units.yml` also asserts app-frontend coverage: every `pillars/*/app` member must route to a covering FE workflow or discovery fails.

`unit-quality.yml` then runs format check, lint, dependency-closure build, typecheck, **codegen drift**, and test for each changed TS unit (and fmt/clippy/build/test for each changed Rust unit). A breaking contract change that ripples into a consumer surfaces as that consumer's typecheck/test failure in the same run, because the consumer is in the affected set.

### Registry generated.ts drift — `registry-generated-quality.yml`

A dedicated gate for the cross-pillar manifest: `mise run build` (so contract deps' `dist/` exists) then `mise run registry`, then `git diff --exit-code -- libs/module-registry/src/generated.ts`. Same regenerate-and-diff shape, isolated because it depends on the whole graph being built first.

### CI Gate aggregator

`ci-gate.yml` aggregates the required lanes (including `unit-quality`) into one required status check, so a drifted artifact blocks merge.

## Rules

- **Committed artifacts must be a pure projection of source.** On every PR, for every changed unit, CI regenerates and diffs. A hand-edited or stale `*.generated.ts` / `*.openapi.json` fails the build with "run the unit's `generate:*` scripts and commit the result."
- **Generation is deterministic.** Sorted keys + formatter pass. No spurious diffs from ordering or environment.
- **A unit declares its own generators.** The gate runs exactly the `generate:*` scripts the unit's `package.json` defines — no central registry of what to regenerate. Adding a generator to a pillar automatically enrolls it in the gate.
- **Affected scope is computed against the PR's merge-base**, not `main`'s tip, so the changed set stays anchored to the branch's actual base even as `main` advances.
- **A shared-root change rebuilds everything.** A lockfile, base tsconfig, formatter-config, or toolchain-config touch can affect any unit, so all units enter the changed set.
- **Selection is by manifest name, never dir basename.** `pnpm --filter` exits 0 (a warning) on zero matches, which would silently run nothing; `unit-quality.yml` asserts `--filter` matches exactly one package and fails loud otherwise.
- **Consumer breakage blocks the contract PR.** Because affected consumers are in the same matrix, a breaking contract change that isn't paired with consumer migration fails the consumer's typecheck/test.
- **The registry manifest is diff-gated separately** (`registry-generated-quality.yml`) because it must build the full graph first.

## Edge cases

| Case                                                                                  | Behaviour                                                                                                                                                                         |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Author edits `src/contract/rest.ts` but forgets to regenerate the OpenAPI / api-types | Codegen drift step regenerates and `git diff --exit-code` fails; PR blocked until the artifacts are committed.                                                                    |
| Author hand-edits a `*.generated.ts` artifact                                         | Regeneration overwrites the hand edit; the diff fails. Generated files are not editable by hand.                                                                                  |
| Generator output ordering is nondeterministic across machines                         | Disallowed by construction — generators sort keys recursively and run the formatter, so local and CI output match byte-for-byte.                                                  |
| Pillar defines no `generate:*` scripts                                                | Codegen drift step prints "no generate:\* scripts — skip" and passes.                                                                                                             |
| `main` advances between PR open and PR check                                          | Affected scope is anchored to `git merge-base origin/<base> HEAD`, so the changed set is unaffected.                                                                              |
| A lockfile / base-tsconfig / formatter-config change                                  | Shared-root invalidation puts every unit in the changed set; the whole matrix runs.                                                                                               |
| Dir basename differs from manifest name                                               | `--filter` would match zero packages; the "exactly one package" assertion fails loud rather than silently skipping.                                                               |
| Contract change ripples into a consumer in the same PR                                | The consumer is in the affected set; its typecheck/test runs and fails if the change broke it, blocking the contract PR.                                                          |
| Rust pillar (`contacts`) emits OpenAPI from axum, not ts-rest                         | Same committed-artifact rule; the rust lane builds/tests it, and its OpenAPI drift is checked the same way.                                                                       |
| Registry `generated.ts` goes stale because a pillar's manifest changed                | `registry-generated-quality.yml` rebuilds the graph, regenerates, and diffs `libs/module-registry/src/generated.ts`; stale state fails with "run `mise run registry` and commit." |

## Acceptance criteria

- [x] Each TS contract-serving pillar commits its OpenAPI document (`openapi/<id>.openapi.json`) and `src/contract/api-types.generated.ts`, with `generate:openapi` + `generate:api-types` scripts that re-derive them. Pillars that declare `generate:manifest` (`cerebrum`, `food`, `inventory`, `lists`) also commit a drift-gated `manifest.generated.ts`.
- [x] Generators are deterministic (recursively sorted keys + formatter pass) so regenerate-and-diff is byte-stable.
- [x] `unit-quality.yml` runs every changed unit's `generate:*` scripts and fails the job on any `git diff` in the unit directory.
- [x] Units with no `generate:*` scripts skip the codegen-drift step and pass.
- [x] The affected set is computed against `git merge-base origin/<base> HEAD`, not `main`'s tip.
- [x] A shared-root file change (lockfile, base tsconfig, formatter/lint/toolchain config) invalidates all units.
- [x] Unit selection is by manifest `name`, with an "exactly one package matched" assertion that fails loud on a basename/name skew.
- [x] A contract change that breaks a consumer fails the consumer's typecheck/test in the same CI run (consumers are in the affected set).
- [x] `libs/module-registry/src/generated.ts` has its own regenerate-and-diff drift gate (`registry-generated-quality.yml`).
- [x] The drift lanes feed the required `ci-gate.yml` aggregator so a drifted artifact blocks merge.

## Not built (see idea)

Semantic-version classification and enforcement of the wire surface — diffing a contract's public TS + Zod surface against a baseline, classifying the change as patch/minor/major, and hard-failing a PR whose declared version doesn't match — was specified but never built. There are no per-contract `package.json` version bumps wired to CI, no `contract-<pillar>@v<semver>` git tags, no `api-extractor` TS-surface diff, no Zod `_def` diff, no per-contract `CHANGELOG.md` migration enforcement, and no self-test fixture. All pillar `version` fields sit at `0.1.0` and are not load-bearing. See [docs/ideas/contract-semver-ci.md](../../../ideas/contract-semver-ci.md).
